twitter.label: Twitter/X
twitter.structure: Thread format if multi-post, numbered or unnumbered continuation
twitter.cta: Question or bold statement to drive engagement
rules: Hook in first post (question, bold claim, stat)
No hashtag spam (0-2 max)
Sentence fragments normal
No em dashes

linkedin.label: LinkedIn
linkedin.structure: Hook-first with 2-line opener visible before 'see more', body in short paragraphs, close with CTA or question
linkedin.cta: Question to invite comments or direct call-to-action
rules: First 2 lines must compel the click to 'see more'
1-2 sentences per paragraph, generous white space
Emojis sparingly OK (0-3 total)
No hashtag spam (2-4 relevant tags at end)
Personal anecdote or data point in opening

blog.label: Blog Post
blog.structure: Hook within first 100 words, H2/H3 headings every 200-300 words, scannable body, conclusion with takeaway
rules: Opening hook within first 100 words
H2 or H3 subheading every 200-300 words
Links inline, not listed at end
Meta description (155 chars) as a separate output
Avoid passive voice where possible
One idea per paragraph

whitepaper.label: Whitepaper
whitepaper.structure: Executive summary required, numbered sections, data tables and charts referenced inline, citations throughout
whitepaper.cta: Recommendations section or contact information at close
rules: Executive summary required (250-400 words)
Numbered sections for navigation
All data tables and charts must be referenced in body text
Citations in chosen style (APA default)
No first-person singular, prefer 'we' or passive construction
Define all acronyms on first use

email.label: Email
email.structure: Subject line + preview text, front-loaded ask in first 2 sentences, scannable body, single CTA
email.cta: Single specific action, button or linked text
rules: Subject line: 6-10 words, specific not generic
Front-load the ask or key point in first 2 sentences
Use bullets if listing more than 3 points
One CTA only — no competing actions
Preview text should complement subject line, not repeat it
Sign-off matches relationship formality

tiktok.label: TikTok
tiktok.structure: Hook in first 80 chars (visible before expand), narrative body (never listicle format in caption), hashtags on final line
tiktok.tone: Personal, specific, enthusiastic about the craft
tiktok.cta: Spoken CTA in video + written CTA in caption (save, comment keyword for DM)
rules: First 80 chars are the hook — must work as standalone text
On-screen text indexed for search — caption complements, does not duplicate
Narrative structure required — never write caption as a bulleted list
3-5 hashtags on separate final line
Duration sweet spot 60-90s for resource content, 30-60s for demos

instagram-reel.label: Instagram Reel
instagram-reel.structure: Keywords in first line (outperform hashtags for discovery), body with context, hashtags at end
instagram-reel.cta: Save CTA in caption, comment CTA if using DM automation
rules: First 125 chars visible without tap — front-load keywords here
Keywords in first line outperform hashtags for Instagram search
5 hashtag hard cap — every tag must earn its slot
Sends (DM shares) weigh more than likes in Instagram algorithm
Use Trial Reels at 1K+ followers to test with non-followers first

instagram-carousel.label: Instagram Carousel
instagram-carousel.structure: First slide is hook (must work at thumbnail size), body slides deliver value, final slide is save/follow CTA
instagram-carousel.cta: Final slide: save + follow CTA. Caption: keyword-rich summary.
rules: First slide IS the hook — text must be readable at thumbnail size
5-10 slides optimal for listicle or comparison format
Final slide always includes save/follow CTA
Add music to push carousel into Reels feed
Save-optimized format — design for bookmarking, not just scrolling

youtube-short.label: YouTube Short
youtube-short.structure: Searchable title in 'How to' format, evergreen framing, 60s max duration
youtube-short.cta: Subscribe CTA, link to full video if applicable
rules: Title must be searchable — 'How to [X] with [Y]' format preferred
Evergreen framing — avoid time-sensitive references
60s maximum duration (platform limit)
74% of Short views come from non-subscribers — optimize for discovery
Description supports SEO — include relevant keywords
